title: Track Usage of Disallowed Methods in Java
description: This rule detects and prevents the usage of certain disallowed methods in your Java code. This is essential to ensure that only compliant methods that maintain the integrity and security of your codebase are being used.
recommendation: Avoid using the disallowed methods in your Java code. Always adhere to the allowed methods for your project's coding guidelines.
impact: Using disallowed methods can potentially introduce vulnerabilities into your code. It can lead to issues such as unexpected behavior, security vulnerabilities, and maintainability problems.

noncompliant:
className:java.lang.String
argumentTypes: java.lang.CharSequence, java.lang.CharSequence
name.replace("A","a");  // Noncompliant

checks.message: Avoid using the disallowed String.replace() methods in your Java code. Always adhere to the allowed methods for your project's coding guidelines.
